extra.reason: Posted page to https://wikihub.md/@jacobcole/team-strategy/conversations/2026-04-29-jacob-tejas-open-threads — split into (A) 8 open company/strategic threads and (B) 21 open projects with pending design decisions, plus cross-cutting and ruled-out items.

Shipped: app/backlinks.py shared module, GET /api/v1/wikis/<owner>/<slug>/pages/<path>/backlinks endpoint, ?include=backlinks on the page-read endpoint, partial indexes (migrations/2026-04-29_wikilinks_backlink_indexes.sql), and forward-ref alias fallback so backlinks appear even when the source linked to a target before it existed. e2e test 'backlinks API + forward-ref fallback' covers all four paths. Reader UI was already wired (templates/reader.html backlinks-block); _get_backlinks now delegates to the shared module so API and view are in lockstep. Cross-wiki backlinks remain out of scope (separate ticket if/when needed). 58/58 e2e pass; live smoke verified on dev server.
Verified already shipped: auth/login.html already uses the [[wikihub]] logo as a home link, matching the ticket description.

Implemented next-preserving sign-in redirects for password, API-key, Google, and magic-link flows; switched browser-facing wiki auth denials to permission_error where needed; added the e2e regression test and verified the running dev server flow.

Fixed in c12a073: default .search-trigger CSS in base.html changed from display:none to inline-flex so the search button shows at every viewport. Below 640px we collapse the 'Search' label + ⌘K kbd so only the icon shows (40x40 touch target). Same pattern applied to landing.html's embedded CSS for authed users. Added regression test test_search_trigger_visible_on_mobile that parses base.html and fails if the default block has display:none. Verified in Chrome DevTools at 390x844 iPhone, 768x1024 iPad portrait, and 1024x1366 iPad landscape against both dev (http://localhost:5100) and prod (https://wikihub.md/) — search button visible in all cases. Deployed to prod 2026-05-30 via git pull + systemctl restart wikihub.

Root cause was NOT a CF rule/page/worker — DNS A records for wikihub.md and *.wikihub.md still pointed to stale GCP origin 35.237.163.58 (Google Cloud, North Charleston SC). That origin serves a static 9222-byte welcome.html with HTTP 200 for ALL paths. The wikihub-fg1p fix landed on the AWS Lightsail origin 54.145.123.7 but no traffic reached it. Updated both CF DNS A records to 54.145.123.7, purged cache, enabled+disabled development_mode to flush. Verified via curl: HTTP/2 404 for nonexistent paths, HTTP/2 200 with Sign in link on /, HTTP/2 404 with permission_error template + Sign in on private wiki pages unauth. Backup of pre-change DNS in ~/memory/machines/mac-mini/backups/wikihub-cf-dns-pre-2026-05-30.json

Origin nginx fix landed in commit a3264de + live nginx reload on prod. wikihub-ju4w (CF DNS to stale GCP origin) was the missing piece — now resolved by repointing CF A records to 54.145.123.7. Verified prod: HTTP/2 404 on private/unknown paths, HTTP/2 200 on landing, permission_error.html + Sign in served correctly.

Resolved by wikihub-ju4w DNS fix. The OLD welcome.html (9222 bytes, no Sign in) was being served from the stale GCP origin 35.237.163.58. The current welcome.html (deploy/static/welcome.html, ~62KB) ALREADY contains a /auth/login Sign in link in the nav. Now that traffic reaches AWS Lightsail, users see the correct page. Verified curl on prod / shows Sign in.
Fixed by wikihub-fg1p + wikihub-ju4w. Verified prod: GET /totally-nonexistent-path-zzz returns HTTP/2 404, branded 'Page not found — wikihub' page via base.html, with /auth/login?next= Sign in link.

Fixed by wikihub-fg1p + wikihub-ju4w. Verified prod: unknown URLs return HTTP 404 (not 200), private content returns 404 with Sign in. Sitemap/robots audit deferred — sitemap.xml/robots.txt currently return 404, can file separate ticket if needed.

Mobile hamburger landed in commit 423e848 + base.html CSS. nav-hide-mobile breakpoint extended to 1024px (covers iPad portrait 768px). Hamburger button (44px touch target, top-right) opens a slide-out panel with Explore/People/For Agents/My Wiki/Shared/Settings/Logout (auth) or Sign in (anon). Esc closes, backdrop click closes, body scroll locked while open. Test: 'mobile hamburger exposes hidden nav (wikihub-pz27)' in tests/test_e2e.py.

Closed in commits 423e848 + 84c5bd5 + 7089a03. Wiki page handler returns JSON 4xx with WWW-Authenticate: Bearer realm='wikihub' + sign_in_url when Accept includes text/markdown or URL ends .md without text/html Accept. Three layers: (1) can_read deny in wiki_page route, (2) content-is-None fallback in wiki_page, (3) global 404 errorhandler in __init__.py for abort(404) callers. Verified prod: curl -H 'Accept: text/markdown' wikihub.md/@jacobcole/otter-highlights/2026-05-29/SUMMARY.md returns HTTP/2 401, content-type: application/json, www-authenticate: Bearer realm='wikihub'. Test: 'md request for private page returns 4xx (wikihub-3rjt)'.

Closed in commit 423e848. New routes in app/routes/api_root.py: GET /api/wikis/<owner>/<slug> and GET /api/wikis/<owner>/<slug>/pages/<path>. Unauth to private wiki returns 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Bearer realm='wikihub' + JSON {error: 'authentication_required', message, sign_in_url: 'https://wikihub.md/auth/login', hint}. Owner authed gets 200 with canonical_api + web_url hints (canonical_api: /api/v1/wikis/<owner>/<slug>). 404 includes discovery hint pointing at /api version map. Verified prod: HTTP/2 401, json body parsed cleanly, WWW-Authenticate header present. Test: '/api/wikis 401+WWW-Authenticate (wikihub-uonp)'.

Audit ticket — core acceptance criteria met by existing scope chip (search.js + .search-scope-pill CSS) and the new regression test confirming logged-out search returns only public content. The remaining e8mm/lvrc are separate UX scope-selection epics, not blockers for this audit. Test: 'logged-out search returns only public (wikihub-7dml)'.

Set WTF_CSRF_SSL_STRICT=False in app/__init__.py (commit bba85c1); regression test test_login_post_without_referer_succeeds_with_csrf_token_wikihub_m8zi added (71 passed, 0 failed); deployed to wikihub-prod GCP (us-east1-b); verified prod POST /auth/login without Referer returns 302 (was 400); iPad emulation in Chrome DevTools (768x1024, iOS 17.5 UA): API-key login succeeded, redirected to https://wikihub.md/ with Account menu visible. CSRF token check still enforced (token-less POST still returns 400).

Fixed in commit 1596239: replaced min-height:calc(100vh-56px)+justify-content:center on .error-page with top-anchored clamp-based padding; added flex-wrap:wrap on .error-actions and min-width:120px on .btn. Regression test in tests/test_e2e.py:test_error_page_ipad_alignment_fix. Deployed to wikihub-prod GCP, service active, smoke tests green.

Fixed in 9e54a18: base.html script src switched to @highlightjs/cdn-assets@11.9.0/highlight.min.js. Verified prod returns 200 + application/javascript; iPad Chrome DevTools shows hljs 11.9.0 loaded, 3 code blocks all with class='hljs' data-highlighted='yes' and hljs-section/hljs-code tokens rendered. Regression test added (test_highlight_js_script_url_is_canonical).

Fixed in 4505095 + 775680f: nginx adds Service-Worker-Allowed: / and Cache-Control: no-store for /static/sw.js in both wikihub.md server blocks. Verified prod headers present (cf-cache-status: BYPASS). iPad Chrome DevTools shows SW registered with scope: https://jacobcole.wikihub.md/, state: activated, zero console errors. Regression test added (test_nginx_serves_service_worker_allowed_header).
